hero.title: I design and operate production systems for e-commerce automation.
hero.description: Technical founder with 5+ years of experience. I built a full ERP, reverse-engineered private APIs, migrated production databases, and integrated physical hardware into cloud workflows — while running a real business with a team of 6.

about.title: About Me
about.p1: I founded a company at 25, built its entire tech stack from zero, and operated it for 3+ years — processing thousands of SKUs across multiple marketplaces, managing a growing team (now 6), and handling everything from warehouse logistics to AI-powered product enrichment.
about.p2: Before that, I cut my teeth at enterprise consultancies (Capgemini, Sopra Steria, LittleBig Connection), working on systems for AIRBUS and Mercadona — learning what scale, reliability, and stakeholder management really mean.
about.p3: In October 2024, a catastrophic flood destroyed our warehouse. I directed 60 people in recovery, rebuilt the company from scratch in 2 months, and used the crisis to build everything better — proper database, proper ERP, proper documentation. Read the full story →
about.p4: I'm not a tutorial developer. Every line of code I write handles real inventory, real money, and real operations. I've reverse-engineered private APIs, migrated production databases, integrated physical printers into cloud workflows, and built AI pipelines that run autonomously in production.
about.d_architect: End-to-end platform design — from database schema to cloud deployment to hardware integration.
about.d_founder: Built and ran a real business — team management, operations, and full-stack development simultaneously.
about.d_solver: Reverse engineering, data migration, anti-detection — I solve problems others avoid.

experience.title: Experience

experience.reusalia_3: Built multi-marketplace automation (Wallapop, eBay, PortalHero) with AI enrichment pipeline
experience.reusalia_4: Reverse-engineered private APIs, built anti-detection systems, integrated label printers
experience.reusalia_5: Survived DANA floods (Oct 2024) — rebuilt company from zero, grew team from 3 to 5
experience.reusalia_6: Migrated production data from Google Sheets → PostgreSQL (5-phase pipeline)

experience.sopra_2: Re-architected Angular state management (NgRx), eliminating UI rendering freezes

experience.capgemini_3: Primary technical interface for Europe's largest grocery supply chain

architecture.title: System Architecture
architecture.subtitle: The full production system I designed, built, and operated — from marketplace APIs to physical label printing.
architecture.bridge_desc: Exclusive lock · Heartbeat monitoring · ~60s automatic failover

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Components of a production e-commerce platform — built, iterated, and operated over 3 years.
projects.flagship_desc: End-to-end e-commerce automation: reverse-engineered APIs, AI enrichment, multi-marketplace sync, ERP, and hardware integration — designed, built, and operated by one engineer.
projects.ai_enrichment_desc: Automated pipeline: Amazon scraping → GPT categorization → description generation → listing sync. Processes thousands of SKUs autonomously in production.
projects.wallapop_desc: Reverse-engineered private API. Multi-account extraction with cookie auth, anti-detection, parallel processing. 2,100+ lines, 5 major versions, ~100 orders/day across 12 accounts.
projects.ebay_desc: End-to-end eBay automation: OAuth2 auth, bulk CSV generation, Feed API uploads, order import, shipping with tracking.
projects.inventory_desc: Full REST API for e-commerce ops: items, orders, sales, invoicing, scheduler, cookie management, label printing integration. The backbone of the entire REUSALIA platform.
projects.bridge_desc: Distributed pattern: cloud UI enqueues commands via database, local backend processes with exclusive locking, heartbeat, and ~60s failover.
projects.migration_desc: 5-phase production migration pipeline: extract → validate → migrate → sync → verify. Financial reconciliation per Amazon truckload.
projects.sync_desc: Multi-platform inventory sync: single source of truth in PostgreSQL, syncs to Wallapop, eBay, and PortalHero with marketplace-specific formatting.
projects.scraper_desc: Production scraper for Amazon.es: titles, prices, hi-res images, features. Anti-detection with UA rotation and request throttling.
projects.auth_desc: Multi-account authentication lifecycle: cookie/bearer management, JWT decoding, automatic token refresh, secure storage in PostgreSQL.
projects.image_desc: Image processing for marketplaces: download, anti-duplicate transforms (OpenCV), resize, compress, upload to cloud storage.
projects.retool_desc: Internal ERP on Retool: 342 files across 10+ modules — orders, inventory, invoicing, incidents, chat analytics, mobile scanner.
projects.inventory_metric: Used daily by 6 employees · Powers operations across 3 marketplaces
projects.wallapop_metric: Replaces ~5h/day manual work · Makes multi-account ops physically possible
projects.ai_enrichment_metric: Saves ~40h/week vs manual descriptions · Thousands of SKUs processed
projects.ebay_metric: 1,000+ item batch uploads · Full order lifecycle automated
projects.bridge_metric: ~60s failover · Bridges cloud Retool UI to local label printers
projects.migration_metric: 3 years of production data · Zero-downtime cutover
projects.sync_metric: 3 marketplaces synced · Single source of truth for all stock
projects.scraper_metric: Feeds AI pipeline with product data at scale · Autonomous execution
projects.auth_metric: 12+ accounts managed · Automatic rotation across all extractors
projects.retool_metric: 342 files · 10+ modules · Used daily by entire team

blog.title: Technical Writing
blog.subtitle: Deep dives into real problems I solved — technical and otherwise.
blog.migration_title: How I Migrated a Production Business from Google Sheets to PostgreSQL
blog.migration_excerpt: 5 phases, daily syncs, financial reconciliation, and zero downtime — while running the business with 4 employees. The real engineering behind a spreadsheet-to-database migration.
blog.wallapop_title: From Manual Posting to Reverse Engineering: My Wallapop Journey
blog.wallapop_excerpt: 10 min/item manually → Selenium bots across 6 remote PCs → reverse engineering a private API → 2,100-line extraction system processing 100 orders/day across 12 accounts.
blog.bridge_title: Bridging Cloud and Local: A Database Command Queue Pattern
blog.bridge_excerpt: When your cloud UI needs to print a physical label on a USB printer in a warehouse — you need a creative architectural solution.
blog.dana_title: Rebuilding a Company After a Catastrophic Flood
blog.dana_excerpt: The DANA floods destroyed our warehouse. I directed 60 people in recovery, rebuilt from scratch in 2 months, grew the team from 3 to 5, and used the crisis to build everything better.
blog.flagship_title: How I Built a Full ERP That Runs a Multi-Marketplace E-Commerce Operation
blog.flagship_excerpt: One engineer. 180+ scripts. 12 accounts. ~100 orders/day. The complete technical story — from Selenium bots on 6 PCs to a full ERP with reverse-engineered APIs, AI pipelines, and hardware integration.

contact.title: Get In Touch
contact.text: I'm open to freelance projects, consulting, and technical co-founder opportunities. If you're building something interesting and need someone who can architect, build, and operate — let's talk.
